prerequisites: A Nutshell user email address to supply as the username config field.
An API key generated in your Nutshell account settings to supply as the password config field.

authMethods.name: HTTP Basic authentication (email + API key)
authMethods.summary: All Nutshell APIs use HTTP Basic authentication over HTTPS. Set the username config field to a Nutshell user's email address and the password secret field to that user's API key. The connector sends these as the Basic auth username and password.

setupSteps.title: Create a Nutshell API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your Nutshell account and click Settings in the lower-left. Under the Connections heading, click API keys, then click Add API key and choose the permission type. Copy the generated key. This key is used as the password, not your login password.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector credentials
setupSteps.body: Set the required username field to the Nutshell user email address associated with the API key. Set the password field (secret) to the API key you generated. The connector authenticates over HTTPS using HTTP Basic auth with these values.

sources.title: Create API keys for 3rd-party apps - Nutshell Help Center
